novel_features.name: Commute tracker
novel_features.description: Save your commute route and check it each morning — next trains plus disruption status in one call, exits non-zero on disruption for cron scripting.
novel_features.rationale: Requires cross-endpoint join (/departures + /disruptions) over a saved route stored locally — no single Navitia call returns this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs to determine whether a user's regular train is disrupted before suggesting alternatives.

novel_features.name: Disruption digest
novel_features.description: Morning brief of all disruptions across your saved lines, ranked by severity: blocked first, then delayed, then informational.
novel_features.rationale: Fans out /traffic_reports over SQLite-keyed lines and sorts by severity — the API has no aggregated disruption summary endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs a structured disruption summary across multiple lines before planning a day's travel.

novel_features.name: Isochrone station list
novel_features.description: Given a starting point and travel duration, list all train stations reachable within that time.
novel_features.rationale: Combines a live /isochrones GeoJSON boundary with a bounding-box filter on the local SQLite stations table — no single endpoint returns this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs to enumerate candidate destinations within a travel-time budget.

novel_features.name: Accessibility report
novel_features.description: For a planned journey, show elevator and escalator status at each transfer station so you know before you travel.
novel_features.rationale: Chains /journeys with per-stop /equipment_reports calls — no tool in the ecosystem currently does this join.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent is planning travel for someone with mobility needs and needs to flag legs with broken equipment.

novel_features.name: Timetable frequency heatmap
novel_features.description: See how many trains run each hour on a line at a stop — ASCII bar chart showing peak and off-peak density.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates a full-day /stop_schedules response into hourly buckets — Navitia has no frequency endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs to reason about service frequency at a given stop across the day.

novel_features.name: Board export
novel_features.description: Export a full departure board as flat CSV or JSONL for pipeline ingestion — all fields, no formatting.
novel_features.rationale: Calls /departures with high count and emits a pipeline-native flat format distinct from human-formatted board output.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent or ETL pipeline needs structured departure data for downstream processing.

novel_features.name: Vehicle journey trace
novel_features.description: Get the full ordered stop sequence for a specific train service — train's perspective, not traveler's.
novel_features.rationale: Uses /vehicle_journeys endpoint (unused in all absorb tools) to return the complete itinerary a train will follow.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs to resolve a train's full route or populate a schedule database.

novel_features.name: Stop cluster
novel_features.description: Group nearby stops within a radius using Haversine distance — useful for deduplicating station indexes and resolving ambiguous names.
novel_features.rationale: Combines a /places geocode with Haversine computation over the local SQLite stops table — pure local-data intelligence.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs to resolve ambiguous station references or deduplicate stop records.

narrative.headline: Every French train, offline search, and eight commands no other SNCF tool has — the definitive CLI for the Navitia API.
narrative.value_prop: The only maintained Go CLI for France's national rail network. Syncs stations and lines to a local SQLite store for instant offline search, wraps every key Navitia endpoint with --json and typed exit codes, and adds eight novel commands — commute tracking, disruption digests, isochrone reachability, accessibility reports, frequency heatmaps, and more — that no existing tool exposes.
narrative.auth_narrative: Requires a free SNCF API key (register at numerique.sncf.com/startup/api/token-developpeur/). Set SNCF_API_KEY in your environment. The API uses HTTP Basic Auth with the key as username and an empty password — handled transparently by the CLI.

quickstart.comment: Verify auth and connectivity (set SNCF_API_KEY env var first)
Sync French rail stations locally for offline search
Morning disruption brief across all synced lines

troubleshoots.fix: Set SNCF_API_KEY — get a free key at numerique.sncf.com/startup/api/token-developpeur/
Free tier is 5,000 req/day. Use --coverage sncf to scope queries, or sync data locally and use offline commands.
Run `sncf-connect-pp-cli sync stations --coverage sncf` first to populate the local SQLite store.
Try station codes instead of city names: `sncf-connect-pp-cli station search Lyon` to find the exact stop_area ID.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when you need to reason about French train travel: planning journeys, checking real-time departures, monitoring disruptions, or populating a transport database. It is the right tool when you need structured JSON output for downstream agent workflows, or when you need offline station search without a live API call on every query.
trigger_phrases: plan a trip from Paris to Lyon
check departures at Gare du Nord
are there disruptions on the TGV today
which stations can I reach from Bordeaux in 2 hours
